title: Release: CHANGELOG entry, version bump rc.16→rc.17, batched Gemini review + coverage
scope: CHANGELOG.md + all package.json with version field + Gemini review + coverage check
description: Cut the rc.17 release. (1) Finalize CHANGELOG.md `## [2.0.0-rc.17]` section consolidating all four work items (H/R/S/Y) — TASK-004 already added the BREAKING note for externalFixturePath; this task collects the remaining entries (help rewrite, host warning, Codex MCP fix or known-issue). (2) Bump version in root package.json + every workspace package.json from `2.0.0-rc.16` to `2.0.0-rc.17`. (3) Run BATCHED Gemini review across the rc.17 diff (per memory/feedback_review_batching.md — review ONCE at end, not per-task). (4) Run coverage check: `pnpm test --coverage` across affected packages, verify no regression vs rc.16 baseline. (5) If TASK-007 deferred Bug Y, add a 'Known Issues' subsection to the rc.17 CHANGELOG entry pointing at the hotfix plan.

files.change: Consolidate entries: ### Polish (help rewrite, host warning rewrite), ### BREAKING (externalFixturePath removal — already added by TASK-004), ### Fixes (Codex MCP wiring per TASK-007 BRANCH A) OR ### Known Issues (Bug Y deferred per BRANCH B)

files.target: version field in every workspace package

implementation: Read git log since rc.16 tag to enumerate every commit landing in rc.17 (should be ~7 commits = TASK-001..007)
Draft CHANGELOG.md `## [2.0.0-rc.17]` section with subsections: ### Polish, ### BREAKING (preserve TASK-004's entry), ### Fixes OR ### Known Issues (depending on TASK-007 branch)
Bump version: use `pnpm -r exec npm version 2.0.0-rc.17 --no-git-tag-version` (or manual edit if pnpm command misbehaves) across root + all workspace packages
Run BATCHED Gemini review: `ccw cli -p 'Review the diff between rc.16 and HEAD for the rc.17 release' --tool gemini --mode analysis` — capture findings
Address any HIGH-severity findings from Gemini review (or document as deferred)
Run coverage: `pnpm -r test --coverage` (or per-package equivalent) — capture coverage % per affected package
Compare coverage to rc.16 baseline (if recorded) — flag any regression >2%
Final smoke: `pnpm build && node packages/cli/dist/cli.js --version` prints `2.0.0-rc.17`
Final smoke: `node packages/cli/dist/cli.js --help` shows the 装/配/跑 intro from TASK-002

reference.pattern: rc release cadence — version bump + CHANGELOG + batched review (per memory/project_v2_rc_continuation.md)
reference.examples: Recent rc.15 / rc.16 release commits (`git log --oneline | grep 'rc.1[56]'`) — mirror their CHANGELOG structure and version-bump scope

criteria: CHANGELOG.md `## [2.0.0-rc.17]` section exists with subsections covering all 4 work items (Polish, BREAKING, Fixes/Known Issues)
All package.json files (root + every workspace) show `"version": "2.0.0-rc.17"` (verify: `grep -c '2.0.0-rc.17' package.json packages/*/package.json` returns count == file count)
Test command: `pnpm -r test --coverage` passes across all affected packages with no new failures
Gemini review report captured (in session folder or commit message); HIGH-severity findings either fixed or explicitly deferred with rationale
`node packages/cli/dist/cli.js --help` shows TASK-002's 装/配/跑 mental-model intro
Bilingual parity check (re-run from TASK-002): `diff <(grep -oE 'cli\.[a-z.-]+\.description' en.ts | sort -u) <(grep -oE 'cli\.[a-z.-]+\.description' zh-CN.ts | sort -u)` returns empty
Behavioral expectation: rc.17 is publishable — version bumped, changelog complete, tests green, no HIGH-severity Gemini findings outstanding

rationale.chosen_approach: Batch the Gemini review + coverage at end-of-plan rather than per-task (per memory/feedback_review_batching.md) — single review pass on the full rc.17 diff catches cross-task interactions cheaper
alternatives_considered: Run Gemini review per-task (rejected per memory)
Skip coverage check (rejected — coverage regression in a polish RC is unacceptable)
decision_factors: Per-task review is wasteful for a polish RC where each task is small + isolated
Single end-of-plan review surfaces interaction bugs (e.g. did help rewrite break a test that uses the old description string?)
rationale.tradeoffs: Larger review surface in one pass; mitigated by the diff being small (≤7 commits)

manual_checks: Spot-check that CHANGELOG entries match actual git log of rc.17 commits
success_metrics: CHANGELOG section complete with all 4 work items represented

risks.description: Gemini review surfaces a HIGH-severity issue requiring code changes that delay rc.17 ship
risks.mitigation: Address within the same plan if scope ≤30min; otherwise defer to rc.17.1 hotfix and document
risks.fallback: Ship rc.17 with the deferred finding in CHANGELOG Known Issues
